children.text: the identity of persons, including parties to a contract; and
proprietary business information.

children.heading: Disclosure by Federal Government employees
children.label: b Disclosure by Federal Government employees

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), no officer, employee, or agent of the United States shall, without the consent of the packer or other person concerned, divulge or make known in any manner, any facts or information regarding the business of the packer or other person that was acquired through reporting required under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: to agents or employees of the Department of Agriculture in the course of their official duties under this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: as directed by the Secretary or the Attorney General, for enforcement purposes; or

children.label: C
children.text: by a court of competent jurisdiction.

children.heading: Disclosure under Freedom of Information Act
children.label: 3 Disclosure under Freedom of Information Act
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no facts or information obtained under this subchapter shall be disclosed in accordance with .section 552 of title 5

children.heading: Reporting by packers
children.label: c Reporting by packers
children.text: A packer shall report all information required under this subchapter on an individual lot basis.
ensures that the information is published on a national and a regional or statewide basis as the Secretary determines to be appropriate;
ensures that the identity of a reporting person is not disclosed; and
conforms to aggregation guidelines established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: e Adjustments
children.text: Prior to the publication of any information required under this subchapter, the Secretary may make reasonable adjustments in information reported by packers to reflect price aberrations or other unusual or unique occurrences that the Secretary determines would distort the published information to the detriment of producers, packers, or other market participants.

children.heading: Verification
children.label: f Verification
children.text: The Secretary shall take such actions as the Secretary considers necessary to verify the accuracy of the information submitted or reported under part B, C, or D of this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, to the maximum extent practicable, provide for the reporting and publishing of the information required under this subchapter by electronic means.

children.label: i
children.text: present information in a format that can be readily understood by producers, packers, and other market participants;

children.label: ii
children.text: adhere to the publication deadlines in this subchapter;

children.label: iii
children.text: present information in charts and graphs, as appropriate;

children.label: iv
children.text: present comparative information for prior reporting periods, as the Secretary considers appropriate; and

children.label: v
children.text: be updated as soon as practicable after information is reported to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: usage of the system developed under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: interpreting and understanding information collected and disseminated through such system.

children.heading: Reporting of activities on weekends and holidays
children.label: h Reporting of activities on weekends and holidays

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Livestock committed to a packer, or purchased, sold, or slaughtered by a packer, on a weekend day or holiday shall be reported by the packer to the Secretary (to the extent required under this subchapter), and reported by the Secretary, on the immediately following reporting day.

children.heading: Limitation on reporting by packers
children.label: 2 Limitation on reporting by packers
children.text: A packer shall not be required to report actions under paragraph (1) more than once on the immediately following reporting day.
administer or enforce the Packers and Stockyards Act, 1921 ( et seq.);7 U.S.C. 181
administer, enforce, or collect voluntary reports under this chapter or any other law; or
access documentary evidence as provided under sections 49 and 50 of title 15.
